problem.instruction: In consultation with the table in (5), fill in each of the blanks ① and ② with the ONE most appropriate distinctive feature. The feature value ('+' or '－') must be specified. Then generalize the patterns in (4).
problem.passage:
Pairs such as threw-through and brewed-brood used to be distinguished by the presence of /j/ in the former member of each pair and the absence in the latter. In certain environments the /j/ disappeared and accordingly, the pairs mentioned above are homophones in most English accents. The words in (1) underwent the same historical change, /j/-dropping, so they do not have /j/ now.

(1) chute [ʃut], chew [ʧu], juice [ʤus]
rude [ɹud], crew [kɹu], shrewd [ʃɹud], grew [gɹu]

In Modern British English, the words in (2) are losing /j/ and consequently, there is variation in their pronunciation.

(2) enthusiasm [ɪnθjuziæzəm] ~ [ɪnθuziæzəm],
thulium [θjuliəm] ~ [θuliəm]
assume [əsjum] ~ [əsum], sue [sju] ~ [su]
resume [ɹɪzjum] ~ [ɹɪzum], presume [pɹɪzjum] ~ [pɹɪzum]
lewd [ljud] ~ [lud], lurid [ljuɹɪd] ~ [luɹɪd]

However, the words in (3) are not involved in the change.

(3) pure [pjʊɹ], *[pʊɹ]  few [fju], *[fu]
view [vju], *[vu]  cue [kju], *[ku]

On the basis of the data above, we can generalize that /j/-dropping is completed after the coronal consonants with [①], and it is now optional depending on dialects or speakers after those with [②]. However, there seem to be exceptions to this generalization. The words in (4) used to contain /j/, but they do not now, as the words in (1).

(4) blue [blu], *[blju]  flute [flut], *[fljut]
flew [flu], *[flju]  glue [glu], *[glju]

(5) Distinctive features
/p,b,m,f,v/ /θ,ð/ /t,d,n,l/ /s,z/ /ʃ,ʒ,ʧ,ʤ/ /ɹ/ /j/ /k,g,ŋ/ /h/
[labial]    +    -    -    -    -    -    -    -    -
[coronal]   -    +    +    +    +    +    +    -    -
[dorsal]    -    -    -    -    -    -    +    +    -
[anterior]  +    +    +    +    -    -    -    -    -
[strident]  -    -    -    +    +    -    -    -    -

Note: '*' indicates unattested pronunciation.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — 발문 조건 확인
steps.explanation:
음운론(phonology) 영역. j-dropping(yod-dropping) 환경 분석 문제. 두 빈칸은 feature 값(+/-) 포함한 단일 feature. 세 그룹 구별:
(1) j-dropping 완료 자음
(2) j-dropping 선택적 자음
(3) j-dropping 무관 자음
(4) 예외 → 일반화 요구
feature 표(5) 참조하여 (1)과 (2)의 공통 feature 차이 파악.

steps.label: 이론 ↔ 지문 대응 — 자음 분류 및 feature 확인
steps.explanation:
(1) j-dropping 완료 자음: ʃ, ʧ, ʤ (chute, chew, juice) + ɹ (rude, crew)
→ 표(5)에서: ʃ,ʒ,ʧ,ʤ = [coronal +, anterior -, strident +], ɹ = [coronal +, anterior -, strident -]
→ 공통: [coronal +, anterior -] → ① = [-anterior]

(2) j-dropping 선택적 자음: θ (enthusiasm, thulium), s,z (assume, sue, resume), l (lewd)
→ 표(5)에서: θ,ð = [coronal +, anterior +], s,z = [coronal +, anterior +, strident +], l = [coronal +, anterior +]
→ 공통: [coronal +, anterior +] → ② = [+anterior]

(3) j-dropping 무관: p,b,f,v,k,g = [-coronal] → 관련 없음.

steps.label: 후보 소거 — feature 확정
steps.explanation:
① 후보: [-anterior] vs [-strident]
→ [-anterior]: ʃ,ʧ,ʤ,ɹ 모두 포함. 완전 탈락 자음 전체 설명.
→ [-strident]: ɹ은 [-strident]이지만 ʃ,ʧ,ʤ는 [+strident] → ɹ 포함 안 됨. 부적절.
→ ① = [-anterior] 확정.

② 후보: [+anterior] vs [+strident]
→ [+anterior]: θ,s,z,l 모두 포함. 선택적 탈락 자음 전체 설명.
→ [+strident]: s,z만 해당. θ,l 제외 → 부적절.
→ ② = [+anterior] 확정.

steps.label: 확정 근거 + (4) 일반화
steps.explanation:
① = [-anterior] 확정: ʃ[−ant],ʧ[−ant],ʤ[−ant],ɹ[−ant] 뒤에서 j-dropping 완료.
② = [+anterior] 확정: θ[+ant],s[+ant],z[+ant],l[+ant] 뒤에서 j-dropping 선택적.

(4) 일반화: blue, flute, flew, glue → bl-, fl-, gl-은 자음군(consonant cluster). /l/ = [+anterior]이므로 일반화상 선택적 탈락 예상. 그러나 실제로는 완전 탈락(j 없음). 이유: [-coronal] 자음이 /l/ 앞에서 onset cluster를 형성할 때 j-dropping이 완료됨. 즉, cluster 구조 [-coronal]+/l/ 환경이 예외적 완료 조건.

steps.label: 전략 메모
steps.explanation:
음운론 feature 분석 출제 패턴:
① 자음 그룹별 feature 표 참조 → 공통 feature 파악 (표 직접 활용).
② 두 그룹을 구별하는 feature 하나를 특정 (± 값 포함 필수).
③ 예외 패턴은 데이터 관찰 → 새로운 환경(cluster) 명시하여 일반화.
함정: [strident]로 접근하면 ɹ(j-dropping 완료)를 설명 못 함 → [anterior]가 더 포괄적.

key_terms.definition_en: A distinctive feature: [+anterior] consonants are articulated at or in front of the alveolar ridge (dental, alveolar). [−anterior] consonants are articulated behind the alveolar ridge (palato-alveolar, retroflex).
key_terms.definition_ko: [전방성]: [+anterior] = 치조 이전 위치 조음(치음·치조음). [−anterior] = 치조 후방 조음(경구개치조음, 권설음).
key_terms.exam_usage: j-dropping 완료([−anterior]) vs 선택적([+anterior]) 구분 feature

key_terms.definition_en: A phonological process in English whereby /j/ (yod) is deleted after certain consonants. Completed after [−anterior] coronals (ʃ, ʧ, ʤ, ɹ); optional after [+anterior] coronals (θ, s, z, l) in many accents.
key_terms.definition_ko: j-탈락(yod-dropping) — 특정 자음 뒤에서 /j/가 삭제되는 영어 음운 현상. [−anterior] 자음 뒤: 완료. [+anterior] 자음 뒤: 선택적.
key_terms.exam_usage: 지문 전체 주제: j-dropping 환경 분석 → ①② feature 채우기

vocabulary.definition_ko: /j/ 음이 특정 자음 뒤에서 탈락하는 음운 현상
혀끝/날이 조음에 관여하는 자음 특성 — θ,ð,t,d,n,l,s,z,ʃ,ʒ,ʧ,ʤ,ɹ,j
치조 또는 그 앞에서 조음되는 자음 특성
vocabulary.example: [+anterior]: θ, t, d, s, z, l / [−anterior]: ʃ, ʒ, ʧ, ʤ, ɹ

model_answer.essay_content: The words in (4) contain consonant clusters in which a [-coronal] consonant precedes /l/ (e.g., bl, fl, gl). Although /l/ is [+anterior], /j/-dropping is completed when /l/ follows a [-coronal] consonant in an onset cluster. Thus, the generalization is that /j/-dropping is obligatory after a [-coronal] consonant-/l/ cluster, regardless of /l/'s [+anterior] feature.

scoring_criteria.criterion: (4) 일반화: 자음군(cluster) 내 /l/ 앞에서 j-dropping 완료

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: /j/-dropping occurs (is completed) after /l/, the consonant with [+lateral], when preceded by a [-coronal] consonant in an onset cluster (e.g., bl-, fl-, gl-).

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: /j/ is dropped after a consonant cluster consisting of a [-coronal] consonant followed by /l/ with [+anterior]. Although /l/ alone only triggers optional j-dropping, the cluster environment makes it obligatory.

references.author: Roach, P.
references.title: English Phonetics and Phonology (4th ed.)
references.excerpt: Yod-dropping has been completed after ʃ, ʧ, ʤ, and ɹ in all major accents; after s, z, θ, and l, it is variable and accent-dependent.

references.author: Wells, J. C.
references.title: Accents of English (Vol. 1)
references.excerpt: Yod coalescence and yod-dropping patterns differ across accents of English. RP has completed yod-dropping after palato-alveolars and /ɹ/, while remaining variable after /l/, /s/, and /z/.

source.concept_explanation:
Yod-dropping (/j/-dropping) in English (Wells 1982; Roach 2009):

Phonological process: /j/ is deleted after certain coronal consonants.
  (음운 현상: 특정 관상 자음 뒤에서 /j/ 탈락.)

① Completed (obligatory) after [−anterior] coronals:
  (완전 탈락 — [−anterior] 관상음 뒤:)
  ʃ, ʧ, ʤ, ɹ → chute [ʃut], rude [ɹud] ✓  *[ʃjut], *[ɹjud] ✗

② Optional (variable) after [+anterior] coronals:
  (선택적 탈락 — [+anterior] 관상음 뒤:)
  θ, s, z, l → assume [səsum] ~ [sjum], lewd [lud] ~ [ljud]

③ Cluster exception: obligatory j-dropping when a [-coronal] consonant precedes /l/ in an onset cluster (bl-, fl-, gl-), even though /l/ is [+anterior].
  (예외: [-coronal] 자음 + /l/ onset cluster 구조에서 [+anterior]임에도 완전 탈락.)
  blue [blu] ✓  *[blju] ✗

questions.subject: linguistics
questions.connection_note: distinctive feature 표 참조하여 음운 환경 파악하는 동일 풀이 전략
